title: Jinjang berry
content:
{{Top|ga|canon=Jinjang Berry}} {{Plant |image=[[File:JinjangBerries-BFTS.png]] |name=Jinjang berry |designation= |class=[[Berry/Legends|Berry]]{{CSWECite|II|164|jinjang}} |origin=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]] |found= |habitat= |lifespan= |description=[[Color/Legends|Blue]]{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} |edible=*[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other|Chapter 9}} *[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]{{Ewoks|Home Is Where the Shrieks Are}} *[[Human/Legends|Human]] *[[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] *[[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] *[[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] *[[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] *[[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] *[[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] *[[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] *[[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] |inedible= |nutrition= |effects= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Come on, Latara, you know it hardly rains up there. And without the jinjang berries, the baby gubas might starve.|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka|}} '''Jinjang berries''', also known as '''Gin-jang berries''', grew on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] ate some of the [[Berry/Legends|berries]] for dinner, and he later helped the Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]], [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] bring jinjang berries to a [[guba bird]] nest. ==Description== Jinjang berries, or Gin-jang berries, grew from the [[jinjang]] [[Bush/Legends|shrub]], which was [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. They were [[Color/Legends|blue]] in color and could be [[Diet/Legends|eaten]] by [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]], [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]], [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]], [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]], [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], and [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]]s. The berries were typically used in [[Foodstuff/Legends|desserts]] to enhance flavors.{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=item|Gin-jang berries}} ==History== {{Quote|What about dinner? You were supposed to get something to eat.''"
"''I did. The Kagles just made me tobo leaves, jing-jang berries, ''[sic]'' fried mahuga.|Wicket Warrick and Teebo|}} Sometime between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],The events of ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' take place between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] per the reasoning [[Template:SWG timeline|here]]. a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] explored the Forest Moon of Endor and foraged the [[Forest/Legends|forests]] to find jinjang berries. The individual later used the [[Berry/Legends|berries]] to cook [[Ice cream/Legends|ice cream]] in a [[Corellian Fried Ice Cream Fryer]]. [[File:MoreJinjangs-BFTS.png|thumb|left|280px|The Ewoks carrying the berries up the mountain]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{EwoksDate|the mention and appearance of [[jinjang]] [[Berry/Legends|berries]]|[[Home Is Where the Shrieks Are]]" and "[[Battle for the Sunstar]]}} a trio of the brown-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] individuals known as the [[Kagles]] served the [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]], along with [[fried mahuga]] and [[Tobo leaf|tobo leaves]], to the Ewok [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] while on Endor. Teebo later recounted the dinner's dishes to his friend, [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]]. Later that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a group of [[Wokling/Legends|young Ewoks]]—Warrick, [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], Teebo, and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]]—carried a bagful of the berries each up a [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] to a [[guba bird]] nest. Kintaka, whose idea it had been, expressed concern for the baby [[Bird/Legends|birds]], who would be in danger of starving due to the lack of [[Water/Legends|rain]]. She reached the top first, offering the jinjang berries to the gubas, but they instead plucked her from the nest, rose in the air, and dropped her; her weight caused the nest to sag down and then spring up, launching the Ewoks to the bottom of the mountain. ==Behind the scenes== Jinjang berries were first mentioned in "[[Home Is Where the Shrieks Are]]," the third episode in the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], which premiered on [[September 20]], [[1986]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} They later appeared in "[[Battle for the Sunstar]]," the twentieth episode of the season, which first aired on [[December 6]] that same year. The [[Disney+]] subtitles for "Home Is Where the Shrieks Are," added on [[April 2]], [[2021]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-vintage-collection-disney-plus|text=The ''Star Wars'' Vintage Collection Has Arrived on Disney+!}} spell the berries' name as "jing-jang berries," while the subtitles for "Battle for the Sunstar" use "jinjang." Jinjang berries, spelled "Gin-jang," also appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}} Meanwhile, [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' included an entry for the berries' bush, spelled "jinjang." Among the two subtitled spellings, "jinjang" is the lone version that is also reflected in other source material—''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''—so this article uses this as the primary spelling, notes ''Galaxies''{{'}} spelling as valid, and treats the second Disney+ spelling as an error. Jinjang berries were added to ''Galaxies'' as a [[W:c:swg:Foraged Rare Component|rare foraged food component]] for the [[W:c:swg:Corellian Fried Ice Cream Fryer (Schematic)|portable deep fryer]], introduced with the release of "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 9 Notes for April 3 2008|Chapter 9]],"{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67692&month=042008|text=Weekly Picks – 4/3/08 - 04/03/2008}} published on [[April 3]], 2008.{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67688&month=042008|text=Chapter 9: The Fury of Exar Kun}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of jinjang berries}} *{{GalaxiesNGE}} {{ID|Gin-jang berries}} *{{Ewoks|Home Is Where the Shrieks Are}} {{1stm}} *{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II||jinjang}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{SWGWiki|Foraged Rare Component}} {{Interlang |es=Baya gin-jang }} [[Category:Berries]] [[Category:Ewok food]] [[Category:Plants of Endor]]
